Advanced Simulation

Overview

Simulation problems require step-by-step execution of a process, following specific rules and state transitions. These problems test your ability to model real-world scenarios, manage complex state, and implement precise logic flow.

Key Properties

  • Time Complexity: Often O(steps × operations) where steps is simulation length
  • Space Complexity: O(state_size) for maintaining current state
  • Core Idea: Model the process accurately and execute step by step
  • When to Use: Process simulation, robot movement, game mechanics, state machines
  • Key Skills: State management, rule implementation, edge case handling

Core Characteristics

  • State Tracking: Maintain current system state accurately
  • Rule Following: Implement precise rules and transitions
  • Step-by-Step: Execute process incrementally
  • Edge Case Handling: Boundary conditions and special states
  • Optimization: Detect cycles, precompute, or mathematical shortcuts

Problem Categories

Category 1: Robot/Movement Simulation

  • Description: Simulate robot movement following commands
  • Examples: LC 2061 (Robot Cleaning), LC 2069 (Walking Robot Simulation), LC 657 (Robot Return to Origin)
  • Pattern: Track position, direction, and state changes

Category 2: Game/Process Simulation

  • Description: Simulate game rules or multi-step processes
  • Examples: LC 2532 (Time to Cross Bridge), LC 1823 (Find Winner of Circular Game), LC 874 (Walking Robot Simulation)
  • Pattern: State machine with rule-based transitions

Category 3: System/Environment Simulation

  • Description: Model complex systems with multiple components
  • Examples: LC 1701 (Average Waiting Time), LC 1603 (Design Parking System)
  • Pattern: Component interaction and resource management

Templates & Algorithms

Template Comparison Table

Template Type Use Case Time Complexity When to Use
Basic Movement Robot navigation O(steps) Simple movement simulation
State Machine Game mechanics O(steps × rules) Rule-based processes
Event Queue System simulation O(events log events) Timeline-based simulation
Grid World 2D environment O(steps) Spatial simulation

Template 1: Robot Movement Simulation

1class RobotSimulation:
2    """Template for robot movement simulation"""
3
4    def __init__(self):
5        # Robot state
6        self.x = 0
7        self.y = 0
8        self.direction = 0  # 0: North, 1: East, 2: South, 3: West
9
10        # Direction vectors: North, East, South, West
11        self.dx = [0, 1, 0, -1]
12        self.dy = [1, 0, -1, 0]
13
14    def turn_left(self):
15        """Turn robot left (counterclockwise)"""
16        self.direction = (self.direction - 1) % 4
17
18    def turn_right(self):
19        """Turn robot right (clockwise)"""
20        self.direction = (self.direction + 1) % 4
21
22    def move_forward(self, steps=1):
23        """Move robot forward by steps"""
24        for _ in range(steps):
25            new_x = self.x + self.dx[self.direction]
26            new_y = self.y + self.dy[self.direction]
27
28            # Check if move is valid (implement based on problem constraints)
29            if self.is_valid_position(new_x, new_y):
30                self.x = new_x
31                self.y = new_y
32            else:
33                break  # Stop on obstacle
34
35    def is_valid_position(self, x, y):
36        """Check if position is valid (override based on problem)"""
37        return True  # Default: all positions valid
38
39    def execute_commands(self, commands):
40        """Execute a sequence of commands"""
41        for command in commands:
42            if command == 'L':
43                self.turn_left()
44            elif command == 'R':
45                self.turn_right()
46            elif command == 'G':
47                self.move_forward()
48            # Add more commands as needed
49
50        return (self.x, self.y)

Template 2: State Machine Simulation

1class StateMachineSimulation:
2    """Template for state machine based simulation"""
3
4    def __init__(self, initial_state):
5        self.current_state = initial_state
6        self.state_history = [initial_state]
7        self.step_count = 0
8
9        # Define state transition rules (override in subclasses)
10        self.transition_rules = {}
11
12    def add_transition(self, from_state, event, to_state, action=None):
13        """Add a state transition rule"""
14        if from_state not in self.transition_rules:
15            self.transition_rules[from_state] = {}
16        self.transition_rules[from_state][event] = (to_state, action)
17
18    def process_event(self, event):
19        """Process an event and transition state"""
20        if (self.current_state in self.transition_rules and
21            event in self.transition_rules[self.current_state]):
22
23            next_state, action = self.transition_rules[self.current_state][event]
24
25            # Execute action if provided
26            if action:
27                action()
28
29            # Transition to next state
30            self.current_state = next_state
31            self.state_history.append(next_state)
32            self.step_count += 1
33
34            return True
35        return False
36
37    def simulate_steps(self, events):
38        """Simulate multiple steps with events"""
39        results = []
40        for event in events:
41            success = self.process_event(event)
42            results.append({
43                'event': event,
44                'success': success,
45                'state': self.current_state,
46                'step': self.step_count
47            })
48        return results
49
50    def detect_cycle(self):
51        """Detect if we've entered a cycle"""
52        seen_states = {}
53        for i, state in enumerate(self.state_history):
54            if state in seen_states:
55                cycle_start = seen_states[state]
56                cycle_length = i - cycle_start
57                return cycle_start, cycle_length
58            seen_states[state] = i
59        return None, 0

Template 3: Event Queue Simulation

1import heapq
2from collections import defaultdict
3
4class EventQueueSimulation:
5    """Template for timeline-based simulation with events"""
6
7    def __init__(self):
8        self.current_time = 0
9        self.event_queue = []  # Min-heap: (time, event_type, event_data)
10        self.system_state = {}
11
12    def schedule_event(self, time, event_type, event_data):
13        """Schedule an event to happen at specific time"""
14        heapq.heappush(self.event_queue, (time, event_type, event_data))
15
16    def process_event(self, event_type, event_data):
17        """Process a specific event type (override in subclasses)"""
18        # Default implementation - override for specific event types
19        pass
20
21    def simulate_until(self, end_time):
22        """Run simulation until specified time"""
23        results = []
24
25        while self.event_queue and self.event_queue[0][0] <= end_time:
26            event_time, event_type, event_data = heapq.heappop(self.event_queue)
27
28            # Update current time
29            self.current_time = event_time
30
31            # Process the event
32            result = self.process_event(event_type, event_data)
33            results.append({
34                'time': event_time,
35                'type': event_type,
36                'data': event_data,
37                'result': result
38            })
39
40        return results
41
42    def get_state_at_time(self, time):
43        """Get system state at specific time"""
44        # Save current state
45        saved_time = self.current_time
46        saved_queue = self.event_queue.copy()
47        saved_state = self.system_state.copy()
48
49        # Simulate to target time
50        self.simulate_until(time)
51        result_state = self.system_state.copy()
52
53        # Restore state
54        self.current_time = saved_time
55        self.event_queue = saved_queue
56        self.system_state = saved_state
57
58        return result_state

Template 4: Grid World Simulation

1class GridWorldSimulation:
2    """Template for 2D grid-based simulation"""
3
4    def __init__(self, rows, cols):
5        self.rows = rows
6        self.cols = cols
7        self.grid = [[0 for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
8        self.entities = {}  # Track entities and their positions
9
10        # Direction mappings
11        self.directions = {
12            'UP': (-1, 0),
13            'DOWN': (1, 0),
14            'LEFT': (0, -1),
15            'RIGHT': (0, 1)
16        }
17
18    def is_valid_position(self, row, col):
19        """Check if position is within grid bounds"""
20        return 0 <= row < self.rows and 0 <= col < self.cols
21
22    def is_free_position(self, row, col):
23        """Check if position is free (no obstacles)"""
24        return (self.is_valid_position(row, col) and
25                self.grid[row][col] == 0)
26
27    def add_entity(self, entity_id, row, col):
28        """Add entity to specific position"""
29        if self.is_free_position(row, col):
30            self.entities[entity_id] = (row, col)
31            self.grid[row][col] = entity_id
32            return True
33        return False
34
35    def move_entity(self, entity_id, direction):
36        """Move entity in specified direction"""
37        if entity_id not in self.entities:
38            return False
39
40        curr_row, curr_col = self.entities[entity_id]
41        dr, dc = self.directions[direction]
42        new_row, new_col = curr_row + dr, curr_col + dc
43
44        if self.is_free_position(new_row, new_col):
45            # Clear old position
46            self.grid[curr_row][curr_col] = 0
47            # Set new position
48            self.grid[new_row][new_col] = entity_id
49            self.entities[entity_id] = (new_row, new_col)
50            return True
51        return False
52
53    def simulate_moves(self, entity_id, moves):
54        """Simulate sequence of moves for entity"""
55        path = [self.entities.get(entity_id)]
56
57        for move in moves:
58            success = self.move_entity(entity_id, move)
59            if success:
60                path.append(self.entities[entity_id])
61            else:
62                break  # Stop on invalid move
63
64        return path
65
66    def get_neighbors(self, row, col, include_diagonal=False):
67        """Get valid neighbor positions"""
68        neighbors = []
69        directions = [(0,1), (0,-1), (1,0), (-1,0)]
70        if include_diagonal:
71            directions.extend([(1,1), (1,-1), (-1,1), (-1,-1)])
72
73        for dr, dc in directions:
74            new_row, new_col = row + dr, col + dc
75            if self.is_valid_position(new_row, new_col):
76                neighbors.append((new_row, new_col))
77
78        return neighbors

Problems by Pattern

Robot Movement Problems

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty
Robot Return to Origin 657 Simple position tracking Easy
Walking Robot Simulation 874 Direction + obstacle handling Easy
Walking Robot Simulation II 2069 Circular path optimization Medium
Number of Spaces Cleaning Robot Cleaned 2061 Grid traversal simulation Medium

Game Simulation Problems

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty
Find Winner of Circular Game 1823 Josephus problem simulation Medium
Time to Cross a Bridge 2532 Priority queue simulation Hard
Snakes and Ladders 909 BFS + board simulation Medium

System Simulation Problems

Problem LC # Key Technique Difficulty
Design Parking System 1603 Resource management Easy
Average Waiting Time 1701 Queue simulation Medium
Design Phone Directory 379 State management Medium

LC Examples

2-1) Walking Robot Simulation II (LC 2069) — Circular Perimeter State Machine

Map position to perimeter index (mod perimeter); track direction at each corner.

1// LC 2069 - Walking Robot Simulation II
2// IDEA: Map perimeter as 1D array (mod perimeter); corners change direction
3// time = O(1) per step, space = O(1)
4class Robot {
5    int w, h, perimeter, pos = 0;
6    // perimeter positions: 0=bottom, w-1=bottom-right, w+h-2=top-right, 2w+h-3=top-left
7    String[] DIRS = {"East","North","West","South"};
8    int[] dx = {1,0,-1,0}, dy = {0,1,0,-1};
9    public Robot(int width, int height) {
10        w = width; h = height;
11        perimeter = 2 * (w + h - 2);
12    }
13    public void step(int num) { pos = (pos + num) % perimeter; }
14    public int[] getPos() {
15        if (pos < w)            return new int[]{pos, 0};
16        if (pos < w + h - 1)   return new int[]{w - 1, pos - w + 1};
17        if (pos < 2*w + h - 2) return new int[]{2*w + h - 3 - pos, h - 1};
18        return new int[]{0, 2*w + 2*h - 4 - pos};
19    }
20    public String getDir() {
21        if (pos == 0)             return pos == 0 ? "East" : "South"; // start facing East
22        if (pos < w)              return "East";
23        if (pos < w + h - 1)     return "North";
24        if (pos < 2*w + h - 2)   return "West";
25        return "South";
26    }
27}

1class Robot:
2    """Optimized robot simulation with circular path detection"""
3
4    def __init__(self, width, height):
5        self.width = width
6        self.height = height
7
8        # Robot state
9        self.pos = 0  # Position on perimeter (0 to perimeter-1)
10        self.direction = 0  # 0: East, 1: North, 2: West, 3: South
11
12        # Calculate perimeter and key positions
13        self.perimeter = 2 * (width + height - 2)
14        self.corners = [0, width - 1, width + height - 2, 2 * width + height - 3]
15
16        # Direction names
17        self.dir_names = ["East", "North", "West", "South"]
18
19    def position_to_coords(self, pos):
20        """Convert perimeter position to (x, y) coordinates"""
21        if pos < self.width:  # Bottom edge
22            return [pos, 0]
23        elif pos < self.width + self.height - 1:  # Right edge
24            return [self.width - 1, pos - self.width + 1]
25        elif pos < 2 * self.width + self.height - 2:  # Top edge
26            return [2 * self.width + self.height - 3 - pos, self.height - 1]
27        else:  # Left edge
28            return [0, 2 * self.width + 2 * self.height - 4 - pos]
29
30    def step(self, num):
31        """Move robot num steps"""
32        if num == 0:
33            return
34
35        # Handle full loops
36        num = num % self.perimeter if self.perimeter > 0 else 0
37
38        self.pos = (self.pos + num) % self.perimeter
39
40        # Update direction if at corner
41        if self.pos in self.corners and num > 0:
42            self.direction = (self.corners.index(self.pos) + 1) % 4
43
44    def getPos(self):
45        """Get current position"""
46        return self.position_to_coords(self.pos)
47
48    def getDir(self):
49        """Get current direction"""
50        return self.dir_names[self.direction]

2-2) Time to Cross a Bridge (LC 2532) — Multi-Queue Simulation

Four priority queues: waiting left/right + working left/right; simulate worker assignments by time.

1// LC 2532 - Time to Cross a Bridge
2// IDEA: 4 heaps (waitL, waitR, workL, workR); pick highest-efficiency waiting worker each step
3// time = O(N log K), space = O(K)  K = workers
4public int findCrossingTime(int n, int k, int[][] time) {
5    // Max-heaps for waiting (by efficiency = leftTime + rightTime, higher = worse = higher priority)
6    PriorityQueue<int[]> waitL = new PriorityQueue<>((a,b) -> b[0]-a[0]); // [eff, id]
7    PriorityQueue<int[]> waitR = new PriorityQueue<>((a,b) -> b[0]-a[0]);
8    // Min-heaps for working (by finish time)
9    PriorityQueue<int[]> workL = new PriorityQueue<>((a,b) -> a[0]-b[0]); // [finishTime, id]
10    PriorityQueue<int[]> workR = new PriorityQueue<>((a,b) -> a[0]-b[0]);
11    for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) waitL.offer(new int[]{time[i][0]+time[i][2], i});
12    int cur = 0;
13    for (int boxes = 0; boxes < n; boxes++) {
14        // Advance time if no one is waiting
15        while (waitL.isEmpty() || waitR.isEmpty()) {
16            int nextFinish = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
17            if (!workL.isEmpty()) nextFinish = Math.min(nextFinish, workL.peek()[0]);
18            if (!workR.isEmpty()) nextFinish = Math.min(nextFinish, workR.peek()[0]);
19            cur = Math.max(cur, nextFinish);
20            while (!workL.isEmpty() && workL.peek()[0] <= cur) { int[] w = workL.poll(); waitL.offer(new int[]{time[w[1]][0]+time[w[1]][2], w[1]}); }
21            while (!workR.isEmpty() && workR.peek()[0] <= cur) { int[] w = workR.poll(); waitR.offer(new int[]{time[w[1]][0]+time[w[1]][2], w[1]}); }
22            if (waitL.isEmpty() && waitR.isEmpty()) cur = nextFinish;
23        }
24        // Priority: right->left over left->right
25        if (!waitR.isEmpty()) {
26            int[] w = waitR.poll();
27            cur += time[w[1]][2];
28            workL.offer(new int[]{cur + time[w[1]][3], w[1]});
29        } else {
30            int[] w = waitL.poll();
31            cur += time[w[1]][0];
32            workR.offer(new int[]{cur + time[w[1]][1], w[1]});
33        }
34    }
35    // Wait for all right-side workers to come back
36    int ans = cur;
37    while (!waitR.isEmpty()) { int[] w = waitR.poll(); ans = Math.max(ans, cur) + time[w[1]][2]; cur = ans; }
38    while (!workR.isEmpty()) { int[] w = workR.poll(); ans = Math.max(ans, w[0]) + time[w[1]][2]; }
39    return ans;
40}

1import heapq
2
3def findCrossingTime(n, k, time):
4    """Simulate workers crossing bridge with priority queues"""
5
6    # Priority queues: (efficiency, worker_id)
7    left_wait = [(time[i][0] + time[i][2], i) for i in range(k)]
8    right_wait = []
9    left_work = []  # (available_time, efficiency, worker_id)
10    right_work = []
11
12    heapq.heapify(left_wait)
13
14    boxes_left = n
15    boxes_right = 0
16    current_time = 0
17
18    def move_available_workers(current_time):
19        # Move workers from work to wait queues when they become available
20        while left_work and left_work[0][0] <= current_time:
21            _, eff, worker_id = heapq.heappop(left_work)
22            heapq.heappush(left_wait, (eff, worker_id))
23
24        while right_work and right_work[0][0] <= current_time:
25            _, eff, worker_id = heapq.heappop(right_work)
26            heapq.heappush(right_wait, (eff, worker_id))
27
28    while boxes_right < n:
29        move_available_workers(current_time)
30
31        # Priority: right to left > left to right
32        if right_wait and boxes_right > 0:
33            # Worker crosses from right to left
34            eff, worker_id = heapq.heappop(right_wait)
35            current_time += time[worker_id][3]
36
37            # Worker goes to put box and work on left
38            work_end_time = current_time + time[worker_id][2]
39            heapq.heappush(left_work, (work_end_time, eff, worker_id))
40            boxes_right -= 1
41
42        elif left_wait and boxes_left > 0:
43            # Worker crosses from left to right
44            eff, worker_id = heapq.heappop(left_wait)
45            current_time += time[worker_id][1]
46
47            # Worker goes to pick box and work on right
48            work_end_time = current_time + time[worker_id][0]
49            heapq.heappush(right_work, (work_end_time, eff, worker_id))
50            boxes_left -= 1
51            boxes_right += 1
52
53        else:
54            # No workers available, advance time to next available worker
55            next_time = float('inf')
56            if left_work:
57                next_time = min(next_time, left_work[0][0])
58            if right_work:
59                next_time = min(next_time, right_work[0][0])
60            current_time = next_time
61
62    return current_time

2-3) Number of Spaces Cleaning Robot Cleaned (LC 2061) — DFS Simulation with State

DFS from (0,0) facing East; stop when revisiting the same (row, col, direction) state.

1// LC 2061 - Number of Spaces Cleaning Robot Cleaned
2// IDEA: DFS simulation; stop when (row, col, dir) state repeats (cycle detected)
3// time = O(M*N*4), space = O(M*N*4)
4public int numberOfCleanRooms(int[][] room) {
5    int m = room.length, n = room[0].length;
6    boolean[][][] visited = new boolean[m][n][4];
7    int[][] dirs = {{0,1},{1,0},{0,-1},{-1,0}}; // E,S,W,N
8    int row = 0, col = 0, dir = 0, count = 0;
9    Set<String> cleaned = new HashSet<>();
10    while (!visited[row][col][dir]) {
11        visited[row][col][dir] = true;
12        if (cleaned.add(row + "," + col)) count++;
13        int nr = row + dirs[dir][0], nc = col + dirs[dir][1];
14        if (nr >= 0 && nr < m && nc >= 0 && nc < n && room[nr][nc] == 0) {
15            row = nr; col = nc;
16        } else {
17            dir = (dir + 1) % 4; // turn right
18        }
19    }
20    return count;
21}

1def numberOfCleanRooms(room):
2    """Simulate robot cleaning with cycle detection"""
3    m, n = len(room), len(room[0])
4
5    # Robot starts at (0, 0) facing right
6    x, y, direction = 0, 0, 0
7
8    # Directions: right, down, left, up
9    dx = [0, 1, 0, -1]
10    dy = [1, 0, -1, 0]
11
12    cleaned = set()
13    visited_states = set()  # (x, y, direction)
14
15    while True:
16        # Clean current position
17        cleaned.add((x, y))
18
19        # Check if we've been in this state before (cycle detection)
20        state = (x, y, direction)
21        if state in visited_states:
22            break
23        visited_states.add(state)
24
25        # Try to move forward
26        next_x = x + dx[direction]
27        next_y = y + dy[direction]
28
29        # Check if next position is valid and not blocked
30        if (0 <= next_x < m and 0 <= next_y < n and
31            room[next_x][next_y] == 0):
32            # Move forward
33            x, y = next_x, next_y
34        else:
35            # Turn right (clockwise)
36            direction = (direction + 1) % 4
37
38    return len(cleaned)

Advanced Techniques

Cycle Detection in Simulations

1def detect_simulation_cycle(state_sequence):
2    """Detect cycles in simulation using Floyd's algorithm"""
3
4    def get_next_state(state):
5        # Implement state transition logic
6        pass
7
8    # Floyd's cycle detection
9    slow = fast = initial_state
10
11    # Phase 1: Detect if cycle exists
12    while True:
13        slow = get_next_state(slow)
14        fast = get_next_state(get_next_state(fast))
15        if slow == fast:
16            break
17
18    # Phase 2: Find cycle start
19    slow = initial_state
20    while slow != fast:
21        slow = get_next_state(slow)
22        fast = get_next_state(fast)
23
24    # Phase 3: Find cycle length
25    cycle_length = 1
26    current = get_next_state(slow)
27    while current != slow:
28        current = get_next_state(current)
29        cycle_length += 1
30
31    return slow, cycle_length

State Compression Techniques

1class StateCompression:
2    """Techniques for compressing simulation states"""
3
4    def hash_state(self, state):
5        """Create hash for complex state objects"""
6        # For tuples/lists
7        return hash(tuple(state) if isinstance(state, list) else state)
8
9    def compress_grid_state(self, grid):
10        """Compress 2D grid into single value"""
11        # Bit manipulation for binary grids
12        result = 0
13        for i, row in enumerate(grid):
14            for j, val in enumerate(row):
15                if val:
16                    result |= (1 << (i * len(row) + j))
17        return result
18
19    def compress_position_direction(self, x, y, direction, max_x, max_y):
20        """Compress position and direction into single value"""
21        return x * max_y * 4 + y * 4 + direction

Optimization Strategies

1class SimulationOptimizations:
2    """Various optimization techniques for simulations"""
3
4    def precompute_cycles(self, initial_states):
5        """Precompute common cycles for faster simulation"""
6        cycle_cache = {}
7        for state in initial_states:
8            if state not in cycle_cache:
9                cycle_start, cycle_length = self.detect_cycle(state)
10                cycle_cache[state] = (cycle_start, cycle_length)
11        return cycle_cache
12
13    def mathematical_shortcuts(self, steps, cycle_length, cycle_start_pos):
14        """Use math to skip repetitive cycles"""
15        if steps <= cycle_start_pos:
16            return steps
17
18        remaining_steps = steps - cycle_start_pos
19        full_cycles = remaining_steps // cycle_length
20        final_position = remaining_steps % cycle_length
21
22        return cycle_start_pos + final_position
23
24    def parallel_simulation(self, entities):
25        """Simulate multiple entities in parallel"""
26        # Use threading or multiprocessing for independent entities
27        pass

Performance Optimization Tips

Memory Management

1def memory_optimization_techniques():
2    """Optimize memory usage in simulations"""
3
4    # 1. Use generators for large sequences
5    def simulate_steps_generator(initial_state, max_steps):
6        current_state = initial_state
7        for step in range(max_steps):
8            yield current_state
9            current_state = get_next_state(current_state)
10
11    # 2. Limit history tracking
12    def limited_history_simulation(state, max_history=1000):
13        history = []
14        while True:
15            history.append(state)
16            if len(history) > max_history:
17                history.pop(0)  # Remove oldest
18            state = get_next_state(state)
19
20    # 3. Use bitwise operations for boolean states
21    def bitwise_state_management(width, height):
22        state = 0  # Single integer instead of 2D array
23        # Set bit: state |= (1 << (row * width + col))
24        # Check bit: (state >> (row * width + col)) & 1
25        # Clear bit: state &= ~(1 << (row * width + col))

Summary & Quick Reference

Common Simulation Patterns

Pattern Template Use Case Example
Robot Movement Position + direction tracking Navigation problems Walking robot
State Machine Rule-based transitions Game mechanics Josephus problem
Event Queue Timeline simulation System modeling Bridge crossing
Grid World 2D environment Spatial problems Robot cleaning

Time Complexity Guide

Problem Type Time Complexity Space Complexity Notes
Basic Movement O(steps) O(1) Simple position tracking
State Machine O(steps × rules) O(states) Rule complexity matters
Event Simulation O(events log events) O(events) Priority queue overhead
Grid Simulation O(steps × grid_ops) O(grid_size) Grid operation complexity

Common Mistakes & Tips

🚫 Common Mistakes:

  • Not handling boundary conditions properly
  • Missing cycle detection for infinite loops
  • Inefficient state representation
  • Forgetting to update all state variables

✅ Best Practices:

  • Always validate moves/transitions before applying
  • Implement cycle detection for potentially infinite simulations
  • Use appropriate data structures for state representation
  • Consider mathematical shortcuts for repetitive patterns
  • Test edge cases thoroughly

Interview Tips

  1. Model the problem accurately: Understand all rules and constraints
  2. Design clean state representation: Easy to update and compare
  3. Implement cycle detection: Prevent infinite loops
  4. Consider optimization: Mathematical shortcuts, precomputation
  5. Handle edge cases: Boundaries, invalid states, empty inputs
  6. Test systematically: Trace through examples step by step

This comprehensive simulation cheatsheet covers the essential patterns and techniques for solving complex process modeling problems efficiently.
